pressure.label: What changed
pressure.prompt: What is making this conversation necessary now?

options.label: Delivery is slowing down important work
options.title: Delivery slowdown
options.summary: The visible problem is not just speed. Current systems may be making it harder to respond to important needs.
firstLook: time required to make a change

options.label: A.I. plans are moving faster than the guardrails
options.title: A.I. guardrail gap
options.summary: The opportunity is real, but adoption may be moving faster than clear ownership, reliable data, and practical safeguards.

options.label: Complexity is growing faster than value
options.title: Growing complexity
options.summary: Systems, subscriptions, custom code, and exceptions may be multiplying faster than the value they create.

options.label: Reliability concerns are getting harder to ignore
options.title: Reliability concerns
options.summary: Confidence is slipping around uptime, security, recovery, or the ability to make changes safely.

constraint.label: What is in the way
constraint.eyebrow: Card 2 of 4 · What is in the way
constraint.prompt: What is making the situation harder to resolve?

options.label: Critical knowledge lives with too few people
options.title: Key-person risk
options.summary: Too much important knowledge is unwritten, difficult to transfer, or held by too few people.

options.label: Ownership is fragmented across teams and vendors
options.title: Split ownership
options.summary: The problem may not have one accountable owner, so local decisions accumulate without a clear overall result.

options.label: Important connections become visible only when something breaks
options.title: Hidden dependencies
options.summary: The systems may be hard to understand as a whole, so each change becomes a partial discovery exercise.

options.label: We do not have enough evidence to choose what comes first
options.title: Missing evidence
options.summary: The organization has options, but not enough trusted information to choose an order, make tradeoffs, or explain the investment.

exposure.label: Where it hurts
exposure.prompt: Where will the problem cause the most damage if nothing changes?

options.label: Customer or staff experience keeps degrading
options.title: Experience degradation
options.insight: The problem is already affecting the people the systems are meant to serve. Friction is showing up in the customer or staff experience.
options.why: When experience degrades gradually, organizations often normalize the symptoms until the cost appears as abandonment, workarounds, or lost trust.

options.label: Daily operations depend on fragile assumptions
options.title: Fragile continuity
options.insight: The system may work today while relying on assumptions that are difficult to test or sustain.
options.why: Fragile continuity is expensive because it is often invisible during normal operation and most costly during an incident or transition.

options.label: Data cannot support confident decisions
options.title: Data trust gap
options.insight: Teams may be moving slowly because the information they rely on is inconsistent, delayed, or difficult to explain.
options.why: When leaders cannot trust the data, automation, A.I., and investment decisions all inherit the same uncertainty.

options.label: Security and control gaps are growing
options.title: Growing control gaps
options.insight: Risk may be spreading through exceptions, unclear access, aging dependencies, or limited visibility into what is happening.
options.why: Security debt compounds quietly. The longer it remains hidden, the harder it becomes to separate urgent fixes from longer-term improvement.

move.label: First step
move.eyebrow: Card 4 of 4 · Choose a first step
move.prompt: What first step would create the clearest evidence?

options.label: Build a clear technology baseline
options.title: Technology baseline
options.move: Begin with a focused technology baseline: map the most important systems, connections, ownership boundaries, and measures before committing to a major program.
firstLook: assumptions about the desired future state

options.label: Contain the most important risk
options.title: Risk containment
options.move: Begin with a targeted containment plan: isolate the highest-consequence risk, define near-term controls, and preserve room for a better long-term decision.

options.label: Run a focused pilot that produces evidence
options.title: Focused pilot
options.move: Begin with a narrow pilot designed to produce evidence: one bounded workflow, one accountable owner, and explicit measures for value, risk, and maintainability.

options.label: Build a practical step-by-step roadmap
options.title: Step-by-step roadmap
options.move: Begin with a practical roadmap: identify the decisions that unlock other decisions, separate reversible steps from commitments, and make the tradeoffs visible.
